conversion_note: v2 seeded conversion of v1 objects/PaymentTerms. The v1 recipe carried a 'discovery' block for REFERENCE_SET, but it was descriptive only -- the v1 CSVs already hard-coded SetCode='COMMON' as a literal (COMMON is the standard seeded reference set, present on every demo pod, 22 seeded subscriptions in the live export). No ${TOKEN} was ever substituted at load time. This v2 recipe DELETES the discovery block and pins SetCode='COMMON' as the seeded reference. Confirmed seeded/no-prefix via read-only BIP: FND_SETID_SETS_VL SET_CODE='COMMON' (SET_NAME 'Common Set') exists; 'ZZ_NO_SUCH_SET' does not. The only templated token is ${PREFIX} on the new term Name (GldRegTerm ${PREFIX} A/B/BAD) and stays exactly as in v1; new terms are naturally re-runnable via distinct names.
proven: v2 seeded LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-20 (see live_evidence). Two good terms reached AP_TERMS_B/_TL/_LINES with real TERM_IDs; the bad term (SetCode ZZ_NO_SUCH_SET) was rejected with a reportable error and absent from base. A second consecutive run with a fresh prefix also passed.

package_layout.structure: FLAT CSVs at the zip root plus the manifest (NO nested batch zip). Members mimic a live setupTaskCSVExports of AP_MANAGE_PAYMENT_TERMS.
members: ASM_SETUP_CSV_METADATA.xml (real, exported live; ProcessType EXPORT->IMPORT). Copied verbatim; not templated.
AP_TERM_LINE.csv (installment lines, FK to header by Name; ${PREFIX} in FK)
AP_TERM_SUBSCRIPTION.csv (reference-set assignment, FK by Name; ${PREFIX} in FK, SetCode HARD-CODED to seeded COMMON / ZZ_NO_SUCH_SET for bad)
package_layout.csv_format: Header row of column names, comma-separated, every value double-quoted, CRLF line endings. Dates YYYY/MM/DD.

service_name: Payables Payment Term service (PaymentTermHeaderVO); the FSM importer resolves SetCode -> SetId for the subscription.

seeded_references.name: REFERENCE_SET
seeded_references.note: Standard seeded reference data set present on every demo pod (FND_SETID_SETS_VL SET_CODE='COMMON', SET_NAME 'Common Set'; 22 seeded subscriptions in the live export). We did NOT load it. No prefix. Confirmed via read-only BIP 2026-07-20. NOT discovered -- pinned as a literal in the template.

good_bad_design.good: Two NEW terms subscribed to the seeded reference set COMMON: 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX} A' (net-30: one line DuePercent=100 DueDays=30) and 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX} B' (net-45: DuePercent=100 DueDays=45). The run ${PREFIX} lives in the term NAME (verification is by name). Standalone reference data - no upstream dependency, nothing discovered.
good_bad_design.bad: A NEW term 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX} BAD' whose AP_TERM_SUBSCRIPTION row names SetCode='ZZ_NO_SUCH_SET' (a reference set that does not exist). The importer cannot resolve it to a SetId and rejects the whole term; it never reaches AP_TERMS_B. Deterministic and pod-independent.

verify.note: AP_TERMS_TL joins to AP_TERMS_B on TERM_ID. The prefix is in the name. Lines are in AP_TERMS_LINES (TERM_ID FK).

base_read_good.sql: SELECT t.NAME AS TERM_NAME, b.TERM_ID AS TERM_ID FROM AP_TERMS_B b JOIN AP_TERMS_TL t ON t.TERM_ID = b.TERM_ID WHERE t.LANGUAGE = 'US' AND t.NAME LIKE 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX}%'

base_read_lines.sql: SELECT b.TERM_ID AS TID, l.SEQUENCE_NUM AS SEQ, l.DUE_PERCENT AS PCT, l.DUE_DAYS AS DAYS FROM AP_TERMS_B b JOIN AP_TERMS_LINES l ON l.TERM_ID = b.TERM_ID JOIN AP_TERMS_TL t ON t.TERM_ID = b.TERM_ID WHERE t.LANGUAGE = 'US' AND t.NAME LIKE 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX}%'
base_read_lines.expect: one line per term: DUE_PERCENT=100, DUE_DAYS 30 (A) / 45 (B)

bad_absent_read.sql: SELECT t.NAME AS NM FROM AP_TERMS_TL t WHERE t.LANGUAGE = 'US' AND t.NAME = 'GldRegTerm ${PREFIX} BAD'
bad_absent_read.expect: zero rows (bad term with invalid reference set rejected, never reaches base)

run_1.bad_error: JBO-27024: Failed to validate a row ... in PaymentTermSubscriptionEO / JBO-27014: Attribute SetId in PaymentTermSubscriptionEO is required
run_1.process_log_summary: A total of 3 rows were processed. 1 rows of them failed
run_1.result: PASS - 2 good terms in AP_TERMS_B/_TL/_LINES; 1 bad row failed and absent

run_2.bad_error: JBO-27014: Attribute SetId in PaymentTermSubscriptionEO is required
run_2.result: PASS - second consecutive run, fresh prefix, distinct TERM_IDs, no collision

live_evidence.rerun_note: New terms are naturally re-runnable: each run stamps a fresh ${PREFIX} into the term Name, so distinct names never collide with prior runs. Two consecutive runs both reached base cleanly (prefix 83810 then 97334).
